notes.title: Business Organization and Summary of Significant Accounting Policies

sub_notes.content: Rytech Franchising, Inc. is a wholly-owned subsidiary of RyBrands, Inc. Incorporated under Georgia law on November 25, 1996. Franchisor of service establishments providing water damage restoration and mitigation/control services.
Financial statements prepared in conformity with GAAP. Management makes estimates and assumptions that affect reported amounts. Actual results could differ.

sub_notes.topic: Initial Franchise Fee and Royalty Fee
sub_notes.content: Nonrefundable initial franchise fee of $60,000 upon execution. 10-year term with certain renewal options and rights to terminate. Franchisees pay monthly royalty based on percentage of gross receipts.

Each franchisee must use Rytech Admin Services for administrative support services (related through common ownership). Services include job dispatching, assignment, invoicing/billing, maintaining accounts receivable, and other administrative services. Franchisees pay monthly administrative fees based on percentage of gross receipts.

Revenue recognition per ASC 2014-09 (Topic 606), Revenue from Contracts with Customers. Royalty fees recognized based on percentage of gross receipts as earned. Initial franchise fees recognized over the franchise term (10 years) as the Company satisfies performance obligations. The standard does not change the timing of royalty fee recognition but changed initial fee recognition from immediate to over the term.
Highly liquid instruments with maturity of three months or less when purchased are considered cash equivalents.

Financial instruments subjecting the Company to credit risk consist principally of temporary cash investments and trade receivables. Cash maintained in bank deposit accounts which at times may exceed FDIC insured limits. Company places temporary cash investments with quality financial institutions.

Advertising and marketing costs expensed as incurred. National Ad Fund: costs totaled $636,156 (2024), $630,170 (2023), $515,307 (2022). Costs incurred by National Ad Fund totaled $515,463 (2024), $382,019 (2023), $451,022 (2022). Florida Cooperative Ad Fund totaled $115,463 (2024).

sub_notes.topic: Method for Determining Allowance for Credit Losses
sub_notes.content: Company extends credit to customers generally without collateral. Collections from customers are continuously monitored. Allowance maintained based on historical experience and reasonable forecasts. Allowance evaluated on portfolio segment basis. Company's allowance for credit losses totaled $180,000 (2024 and 2023) and $170,000 (2022). Credit loss expense of $140,370 during 2022. No credit loss expense for years ended December 31, 2024 and 2023. Net accounts receivable included on balance sheet.
Management evaluated subsequent events through February 27, 2025 (date financial statements were available to be issued). No subsequent events requiring disclosure.
Carrying amounts of current assets and liabilities approximate fair market value because of the short-term nature of these instruments.

notes.title: Income Taxes
notes.content: Company is wholly owned by RyBrands, Inc. and treated as disregarded entity for income tax purposes. All income and expenses reported on tax return of RyBrands, Inc. RyBrands has elected S-corp status under Subchapter S of IRC. Company does not pay corporate income taxes; stockholders are individually liable for income taxes. Financial statements contain no provision for income taxes. Company accounts for income taxes with respect to uncertain tax positions under ASC 740-10. No material uncertain tax positions at December 31, 2024. Tax returns for year ended December 31, 2021 and forward remain subject to examination by relevant taxing authorities.

notes.title: Master Development Agreement
notes.content: Company may grant right to operate as Master Developer. One Master Developer currently exists. Master Developer assists in rendering services to franchisees. Nonrefundable license fee of $100,000 upon execution. 5-year term with certain renewal options. Master Developer entitled to 50% of initial franchise fee and continuing royalty fees paid by each franchisee located in designated territory. Franchise fees paid to Master Developer: $410,174 (2024), $470,071 (2023), $240,159 (2022).

notes.title: National Advertising Fund
notes.content: Established National Advertising Fund to promote goodwill and public image. All contributions used exclusively for these purposes. Franchisees contribute 1% of gross sales. Fund assets and liabilities reported separately.
notes.elimination_note: For financial statement purposes, accounts payable balances payable to the Company were eliminated with the Company's accounts receivable balances for each respective year.

notes.title: Florida Cooperative Advertising Fund
notes.content: Established Florida Cooperative Advertising Fund. Florida franchisees contribute 1% of gross sales. Used exclusively for goodwill and brand enhancement in Florida.

notes.title: Related-Party Transactions
content_items: Company shares office facilities and certain personnel with Rytech Admin Services (related through common ownership). Rytech Admin Services pays all office and personnel expenses and charges Company for estimated proportionate share. These expenses totaled $120,000 (2024, 2023, and 2022).

Royalty fees paid by franchises owned by Company's sole shareholder totaled approximately $85,000 (2024), $96,000 (2023), $145,000 (2022). At December 31, accounts receivable from these franchises totaled approximately $40,000 (2024), $38,000 (2023), $40,000 (2022).

Company incurs operating expenses paid for by companies related through common ownership. At December 31, amounts due to these companies totaled approximately $113,000 (2024), $40,000 (2023), $55,000 (2022).
RyBrands, Inc., sole owner, charges the Company for management administrative services. These fees totaled $1,014,000 (2024), $810,000 (2023), $920,000 (2022).

accounting_policies_summary.revenue_recognition: ASC 606 — royalty as earned, initial fees over 10-year term
accounting_policies_summary.depreciation_amortization: Not applicable (no significant tangible/intangible assets disclosed)
accounting_policies_summary.impairment: Not discussed (no assets subject to impairment disclosed)
accounting_policies_summary.income_tax: Disregarded entity / S-corp passthrough — no corporate tax provision
accounting_policies_summary.lease_accounting: Not discussed (no leases disclosed — home office permitted)
accounting_policies_summary.advertising_fund: Separate accounting for National Fund and Florida Cooperative
